{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Zone Manager",
  "description": "Greenville LNG Company Limited is the pioneer Domestic LNG producer in Nigeria with the objective of extending gas supplies to regions not reached by gas pipelines thereby making available a competitive source of energy to boost the economy. Greenville LNG Plant is in Rumuji near Port-Harcourt and has customer locations in Lagos, Kaduna, Kano, Enugu, Sokoto, Aba and other cities nationwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Zone Manager
Job Locations:&nbsp;Kano, Lagos and Rivers
Job Type: Full-Time

About the job


	Drive and achieve retail sales targets across all existing and upcoming LNG/LCNG stations in Northern Nigeria
	Identify and execute new station rollout and market expansion opportunities
	Ensure efficient and safe operation of all stations with high uptime and service standards
	Manage and grow industrial customer relationships and volumes
	Lead, supervise, and develop regional teams including station managers, operators, and sales staff
	Build team capability through training, coaching, and performance management
	Collaborate with internal teams (logistics, finance, HR, marketing) for seamless operations
	Liaise with external stakeholders including government authorities, regulators, transport unions, fleet operators, and associations
	Enforce discipline, reporting standards, and operational compliance across the region
	Manage regional profitability, cost optimization, and ensure HSE compliance.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Retail Sales Volume: Monthly/quarterly LNG/CNG sales vs. target
	Station Throughput: Average daily sales per station
	Network Expansion: Number of new stations commissioned and operational
	Customer Acquisition: Number of new retail/fleet customers onboard
	Operational Efficiency: Station uptime (%) and turnaround time
	Team Productivity: Sales per staff / station performance metrics
	Receivables &amp; Cash Flow: Timely collections and minimal outstanding dues
	Cost Control: Adherence to regional OPEX budgets
	HSE Compliance: Zero major incidents and audit compliance scores
	Stateholder Engagement: Partnerships with unions, fleets, and local authorities.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in engineering, Business Administration, or related field (MBA preferred)
	15&ndash;20 years of experience in oil &amp; gas, downstream fuel retail, CNG/LNG, or mobility/transport sectors
	Proven experience in retail fuel sales, distribution, or station network management
	Strong exposure to Northern Nigeria market and transport ecosystem preferred
	Demonstrated ability to lead large field teams and multi-location operations
	Experience in stakeholder management including government bodies and transport unions
	Strong commercial acumen with P&amp;L management experience.


Must have skills:


	Leadership &amp; team management, Sales and business development, Operations management Stakeholder engagement &amp; negotiation, Analytical and problem-solving skills, Strong communication and execution capability.

Zone Manager Job at Greenville LNG Company Limited




        
    

        
        Greenville LNG Company Limited is the pioneer Domestic LNG producer in Nigeria with the objective of extending gas supplies to regions not reached by gas pipelines thereby making available a competitive source of energy to boost the economy. Greenville LNG Plant is in Rumuji near Port-Harcourt and has customer locations in Lagos, Kaduna, Kano, Enugu, Sokoto, Aba and other cities nationwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Zone Manager
Job Locations: Kano, Lagos and Rivers
Job Type: Full-Time

About the job
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Drive and achieve retail sales targets across all existing and upcoming LNG/LCNG stations in Northern Nigeria
	Identify and execute new station rollout and market expansion opportunities
	Ensure efficient and safe operation of all stations with high uptime and service standards
	Manage and grow industrial customer relationships and volumes
	Lead, supervise, and develop regional teams including station managers, operators, and sales staff
	Build team capability through training, coaching, and performance management
	Collaborate with internal teams (logistics, finance, HR, marketing) for seamless operations
	Liaise with external stakeholders including government authorities, regulators, transport unions, fleet operators, and associations
	Enforce discipline, reporting standards, and operational compliance across the region
	Manage regional profitability, cost optimization, and ensure HSE compliance.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Retail Sales Volume: Monthly/quarterly LNG/CNG sales vs. target
	Station Throughput: Average daily sales per station
	Network Expansion: Number of new stations commissioned and operational
	Customer Acquisition: Number of new retail/fleet customers onboard
	Operational Efficiency: Station uptime (%) and turnaround time
	Team Productivity: Sales per staff / station performance metrics
	Receivables & Cash Flow: Timely collections and minimal outstanding dues
	Cost Control: Adherence to regional OPEX budgets
	HSE Compliance: Zero major incidents and audit compliance scores
	Stateholder Engagement: Partnerships with unions, fleets, and local authorities.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Business Administration, or related field (MBA preferred)
	15–20 years of experience in oil & gas, downstream fuel retail, CNG/LNG, or mobility/transport sectors
	Proven experience in retail fuel sales, distribution, or station network management
	Strong exposure to Northern Nigeria market and transport ecosystem preferred
	Demonstrated ability to lead large field teams and multi-location operations
	Experience in stakeholder management including government bodies and transport unions
	Strong commercial acumen with P&L management experience.


Must have skills:


	Leadership & team management, Sales and business development, Operations management Stakeholder engagement & negotiation, Analytical and problem-solving skills, Strong communication and execution capability.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "General Manager – Rice Farm and Processing Operations",
  "description": "EcoSusPro is a Nigerian-led, impact-driven organization at the forefront of agricultural development, project implementation, and rural enterprise transformation. We deliver specialist expertise to bridge the gap between time-honored farming traditions and modern agricultural technologies. Through innovative, sustainable approaches that prioritize environmental stewardship and community empowerment, we are transforming Nigeria&rsquo;s agricultural landscape. Our mission is to create replicable, scalable models of rural development that enhance food security, drive enterprise growth, and preserve the environment for generations to come.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;General Manager &ndash; Rice Farm and Processing Operations

Job Location: Delta

The Role&nbsp;


	This is a senior leadership position with responsibility for the entire operation.
	You will lead both the farm and the rice mill, ensuring that production, processing, quality, and delivery are all aligned and performing.&nbsp;
	You will be expected to take ownership, make decisions, solve problems, and build a system that works consistently and efficiently.&nbsp;
	This role requires someone who is comfortable both thinking strategically and operating on the ground.&nbsp;


What You Will Be Responsible For&nbsp;
You will lead the full operation, including:&nbsp;


	Establishing and managing large-scale rice production from land development through to&nbsp;harvest.&nbsp;
	Overseeing the setup and efficient operation of the rice mill, including drying, processing, and packaging. &nbsp;
	Supervising all civil and structural engineering works to ensure compliance with designs,&nbsp;specifications, and timelines.&nbsp;
	Ensuring strong coordination between farm production and processing capacity &nbsp;
	Building and managing a capable and disciplined team across the field and processing operations.&nbsp;
	Putting in place clear systems for planning, tracking, and performance management &nbsp;
	Driving productivity, cost efficiency, and overall operational performance. &nbsp;
	Implementing quality management systems aligned with ISO standards and NAFDAC&nbsp;requirements.
	Ensuring compliance with environmental, health, and safety standards &nbsp;
	Supporting training programmes and developing a pipeline of capable farmers and operators.
	Engaging with communities, partners, and stakeholders to ensure smooth project delivery.


Who This Role Is For&nbsp;
We are looking for a leader who understands how to build and run operations, not just manage them on paper. You should have:&nbsp;


	Demonstrated experience in managing agricultural operations, agro-processing systems, or integrated value chain activities.
	Proven track record of leading multidisciplinary teams and delivering measurable results in complex and dynamic environments. &nbsp;
	Experience in supervising civil and structural engineering works, ensuring compliance with designs, specifications, and timelines.&nbsp;
	Strong practical understanding of production systems, operational planning, and execution discipline.
	Experience in establishing and implementing structured operational systems, including quality management frameworks, ISO-aligned processes, and regulatory compliance such as NAFDAC.
	Clear ability to make sound decisions, take ownership, and consistently deliver outcomes &nbsp;
	Candidates should possess a degree in Agriculture, Agribusiness, Agricultural Engineering, Operations Management, or a related field. Advanced qualifications will be considered an advantage.&nbsp;
	Relevant Professional certifications are highly desirable, including those related to Total Quality Management, ISO standards, food safety systems such as HACCP or ISO 22000, and project or operations management certifications such as PMP or PRINCE2.&nbsp;
	Equivalent practical experience combined with strong leadership capability will also be given full consideration.&nbsp;
	Experience in rice production or processing is highly desirable. However, more important is the ability to lead effectively, build structured systems, and drive execution across the full operation. We strongly encourage applications from qualified women and diverse candidates. We are also open to highly trained and high-potential young professionals who can demonstrate strong leadership ability, practical competence, and the drive to deliver.&nbsp;
	If you have the capability and mindset to lead, we are interested in hearing from you.&nbsp;


Salary
N750,000 - N1,000,000 monthly.&nbsp;

What You Will Gain:


	An attractive and competitive remuneration package &nbsp;
	The opportunity to lead a high-impact, large-scale agricultural operation
	Real responsibility and influence over how the project is built and scaled&nbsp;
	A platform to deliver measurable results and build a lasting legacy&nbsp;
	This role is for someone who wants to build something real. It requires discipline, leadership, and the ability to deliver. If you are ready to take on that responsibility, we encourage you to apply.


Please submit:&nbsp;


	A one-page cover letter outlining your experience and interest&nbsp;
	A CV of no more than four pages
	Two professional references.

General Manager – Rice Farm and Processing Operations Job at EcoSusPro




        
    

        
        EcoSusPro is a Nigerian-led, impact-driven organization at the forefront of agricultural development, project implementation, and rural enterprise transformation. We deliver specialist expertise to bridge the gap between time-honored farming traditions and modern agricultural technologies. Through innovative, sustainable approaches that prioritize environmental stewardship and community empowerment, we are transforming Nigeria’s agricultural landscape. Our mission is to create replicable, scalable models of rural development that enhance food security, drive enterprise growth, and preserve the environment for generations to come.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: General Manager – Rice Farm and Processing Operations

Job Location: Delta

The Role 


	This is a senior leadership position with responsibility for the entire operation.
	You will lead both the farm and the rice mill, ensuring that production, processing, quality, and delivery are all aligned and performing. 
	You will be expected to take ownership, make decisions, solve problems, and build a system that works consistently and efficiently. 
	This role requires someone who is comfortable both thinking strategically and operating on the ground. 


What You Will Be Responsible For 
You will lead the full operation, including: 


	Establishing and managing large-scale rice production from land development through to harvest. 
	Overseeing the setup and efficient operation of the rice mill, including drying, processing, and packaging.  
	Supervising all civil and structural engineering works to ensure compliance with designs, specifications, and timelines. 
	Ensuring strong coordination between farm production and processing capacity  
	Building and managing a capable and disciplined team across the field and processing operations. 
	Putting in place clear systems for planning, tracking, and performance management  
	Driving productivity, cost efficiency, and overall operational performance.  
	Implementing quality management systems aligned with ISO standards and NAFDAC requirements.
	Ensuring compliance with environmental, health, and safety standards  
	Supporting training programmes and developing a pipeline of capable farmers and operators.
	Engaging with communities, partners, and stakeholders to ensure smooth project delivery.


Who This Role Is For 
We are looking for a leader who understands how to build and run operations, not just manage them on paper. You should have: 


	Demonstrated experience in managing agricultural operations, agro-processing systems, or integrated value chain activities.
	Proven track record of leading multidisciplinary teams and delivering measurable results in complex and dynamic environments.  
	Experience in supervising civil and structural engineering works, ensuring compliance with designs, specifications, and timelines. 
	Strong practical understanding of production systems, operational planning, and execution discipline.
	Experience in establishing and implementing structured operational systems, including quality management frameworks, ISO-aligned processes, and regulatory compliance such as NAFDAC.
	Clear ability to make sound decisions, take ownership, and consistently deliver outcomes  
	Candidates should possess a degree in Agriculture, Agribusiness, Agricultural Engineering, Operations Management, or a related field. Advanced qualifications will be considered an advantage. 
	Relevant Professional certifications are highly desirable, including those related to Total Quality Management, ISO standards, food safety systems such as HACCP or ISO 22000, and project or operations management certifications such as PMP or PRINCE2. 
	Equivalent practical experience combined with strong leadership capability will also be given full consideration. 
	Experience in rice production or processing is highly desirable. However, more important is the ability to lead effectively, build structured systems, and drive execution across the full operation. We strongly encourage applications from qualified women and diverse candidates. We are also open to highly trained and high-potential young professionals who can demonstrate strong leadership ability, practical competence, and the drive to deliver. 
	If you have the capability and mindset to lead, we are interested in hearing from you. 


Salary
N750,000 - N1,000,000 monthly. 

What You Will Gain:


	An attractive and competitive remuneration package  
	The opportunity to lead a high-impact, large-scale agricultural operation
	Real responsibility and influence over how the project is built and scaled 
	A platform to deliver measurable results and build a lasting legacy 
	This role is for someone who wants to build something real. It requires discipline, leadership, and the ability to deliver. If you are ready to take on that responsibility, we encourage you to apply.


Please submit: 


	A one-page cover letter outlining your experience and interest 
	A CV of no more than four pages
	Two professional references.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Fina Trust Microfinance Bank Limited
content:
Fina Trust Microfinance Bank Limited was secured from the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) in September 2009 as a Microfinance Bank and commenced operations in October 2009. It holds a State License and hence operates in Lagos State. The registered office address of the Company is 46, Toyin Street, Ikeja Lagos State.

Fina Trust Microfinance Bank has built a resounding success on excellent service delivery in a uniquely customer friendly environment through professional staff, leveraging on world-class technology. A continuous investment in information technology and in people sets the bank apart from others.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Recovery Admin Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Admin Recovery Officer provides administrative and operational support to the Recovery Unit by ensuring effective documentation, tracking, and coordination of recovery activities.
	The role supports collections, legal processes, and asset recovery while maintaining accurate records, monitoring expenses, and facilitating communication across departments.


Key Responsibilities
Reminder & Demand Letters:


	Prepare, issue, and track reminder and demand notices to customers with overdue accounts
	Maintain proper documentation and follow-up records for all communication.


Recovery Expenses Management:


	Record, monitor, and report all recovery-related expenses
	Ensure expenses are properly documented, justified, and approved in line with policy.


Yard & Vehicle Process Management:


	Coordinate and track repossessed vehicles and assets in the recovery yard
	Maintain accurate inventory records and update asset status regularly
	Demonstrate basic technical understanding of vehicles to support asset assessment.


Petition & Legal Case Records:


	Maintain comprehensive records of all legal and petition cases
	Track case progress and update status in internal systems
	Liaise with legal teams on documentation and case follow-ups.


IRR Calculations & Financial Support:


	Assist in performing Internal Rate of Return (IRR) calculations for recovery cases
	Support financial analysis and reporting related to recovery performance


Collection Meeting Coordination:


	Record minutes of recovery/collection meetings
	Track action points and ensure timely follow-up on decisions taken


Follow-ups with Recovery Team Leads:


	Send timely reminders to Recovery Team Leads (TLs) on pending actions
	Monitor deadlines and ensure accountability across the recovery team


Interdepartmental Coordination:


	Liaise with Head Office departments (Legal, Finance, Operations, etc.) on recovery matters
	Ensure seamless communication and timely flow of documents


Reporting & Documentation:


	Maintain accurate databases and records of all recovery activities
	Generate daily, weekly, and monthly reports for management review
	Ensure data integrity and proper filing of all recovery documentation


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Finance, Accounting, or related fields
	2 - 4 years of experience in Recovery and Business administrative support, preferably in banking or microfinance
	Basic understanding of loan recovery processes is an added advantage.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications and resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

2.) Head of Information Technology 

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Context


	we are entering a critical execution phase of its 3?Year IT & Digital Transformation Roadmap. The strategy is defined. The investments are approved. The expectation now is clinical delivery.
	We are seeking a Senior IT business leader—not a theorist—who has the professional weight, regulatory confidence, and technical maturity to execute, enforce, and stabilizea complex digital environment within a regulated financial institution.
	This role shifts deliberately from architect to operator and finisher.
	Success will be measured not by vision decks, but by systems live, projects completed, audits passed, and uptime maintained.


Primary Objective: Roadmap Execution & Delivery


	The Head of IT / CISO is accountable for the end?to?end execution of Fina Trust’s existing 3?Year IT Roadmap, ensuring all milestones are deliveredon time, within budget, securely, and without disruption to active banking operations.


Key Performance Areas (KPAs)
Roadmap Governance & Accountability:


	Translate the approved 3?year roadmap intoquarterly and monthly delivery sprints
	Provideclear, business?focused progress reportingto EXCO
	Proactively identify and resolve technical, regulatory, or vendor risks before they impact timelines
	Ensure that priority initiatives (digital on boarding, app enhancements, infrastructure upgrades) are never stalled by administrative or internal bottlenecks


Agile Operational Excellence:


	Serve as theultimate escalation pointfor Scrum teams and technology partners
	Remove delivery impediments to maintain consistent deployment velocity
	Maintainminimum 99.9% uptimeacross all core and digital banking platforms
	Ensure that speed of delivery does not compromisesecurity, stability, or customer experience


Security, Risk & Compliance Enforcement:


	Function as the Bank’sCISO, embedding security and privacy controls into all roadmap initiatives from inception
	Ensure strict adherence toData Protection and Privacy regulationsand Central Bank requirements
	Maintain continuous audit?readiness, with clean outcomes across internal, external, and regulatory audits


Business & Stakeholder Alignment:


	Act as a senior internal negotiator, aligning IT delivery with the evolving needs of Credit, Operations, and Commercial functions
	Hold third?party vendors to strict SLA performance, initiating corrective action or replacement where delivery is at risk
	Communicate complex IT risks, delays, and achievements in commercial, business?impact languagesuitable for board?level discussions


Required Qualifications & Professional Credentials
Education:


	Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or related field
	MSc or MBA strongly preferred (demonstrated business and executive?level maturity)


Mandatory Certification:


	CISM (Certified Information Security Manager)or
	CISSP (Certified Information Systems Security Professional)


Project & Delivery Credentials (Required/Advantageous):


	Certified Scrum Master (CSM)or equivalent Agile certification (PMI?ACP)
	PMP or PRINCE2strongly advantageous for infrastructure and CAPEX governance


Highly Desirable Credentials;


	CISA(Certified Information Systems Auditor)
	ITIL Foundation or higher


Experience Requirements:


	10–12 years of total IT experience, with minimum 5 years in senior leadership(Head of IT, IT Manager, CISO, or equivalent)
	Proven experience with in Financial Services, Microfinance, or Fintech
	Direct responsibility for IT operations supporting:
	
		Alive digital onboarding platform
		A significant active customer base
	
	
	Demonstrated success delivering at least one full?cycle core banking or digital transformation project—from procurement to stable, high?uptime production
	Hands?on exposure dealing with financial regulators and regulatory audits


The Ideal Profile:


	This role is not for a career technologist seeking safety or predictability.
	The successful candidate is a business leader who uses technology as a weapon for scale and efficiency, is comfortable with personal accountability for uptime, and has a proven reputation as a finisher—someone who takes complex initiatives from“almost there”tostable, secure, and fully operational.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send a detailed resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

3.) Retail Deposit Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Orile, Ajah, Lagos Island, Tradefair, Oyingbo, Agege, Egbeda, Oshodi, Ikorodu, Mile 12, Alaba - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Purpose


	To drive aggressive deposit mobilization, particularly low-cost deposits (CASA), from individuals, traders, associations, and SMEs, while building strong customer relationships and supporting the bank’s liquidity and growth objectives.


Key Responsibilities
Deposit Mobilization (Core Function):


	Source and mobilize deposits from retail customers, market clusters, SMEs, cooperatives, and associations
	Drive low-cost deposit growth (savings & current accounts)
	Achieve assigned monthly and quarterly deposit targets
	Maintain a strong pipeline of prospects through daily field activities


Field Marketing & Customer Acquisition:


	Conduct door-to-door marketing, market visits, and cluster engagements
	Identify new business opportunities within the local community
	Open new accounts in line with bank procedure


Cross-Selling of Bank Products:


	Promote other products such as:
	
		Microloans
		Target savings plans
		Fixed deposits
	
	
	Ensure customers are properly onboarded and educated on product


Reporting:


	Submit daily field activity reports
	Weekly and monthly deposit performance reports
	Track progress against targets


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Deposit mobilization value (monthly/quarterly)
	Number of new accounts opened
	CASA ratio (low-cost deposits)
	Deposit retention rate
	Number of active customers
	Cross-selling performance
	Compliance adherence (zero infractions)


Qualifications & Requirements


	HND/BSc in any relevant discipline with 3–4 years of experience in microfinance or retail banking (deposit mobilization)
	Strong knowledge of local markets and customer segments
	Ability to meet aggressive sales targets


Benefits


	Fixed salary + commission on deposits mobilized
	Bonus for achieving/exceeding targets
	Additional incentives for low-cost deposits (CASAa focus)


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] cc: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Officer",
  "description": "Jubaili Agrotec Limited is a leading agro-allied company dedicated to providing high-quality agricultural inputs across Africa. With operations in Nigeria and several other African countries, the company specializes in crop protection products, fertilizers, seeds, and agricultural equipment. JAG is committed to supporting farmers through reliable products, strong distribution networks, and sustainable agricultural practices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Receive completed manpower requisition form and forward same to Corporate HR
	Coordinate the entire recruitment process, including shortlisting, inviting candidates for interviews, conducting written tests, and facilitating both virtual and physical interviews.
	Upon selection of successful candidates, coordinate pre-employment medical examinations and follow up with the laboratory for results.
	Collaborate with depots to monitor their recruitment activities and provide guidance on outstanding documentation and processes.
	Sought approval from Corporate HR for Salary package and relate same to suitable candidate
	Prepare and issue appointment or contract letters to qualified candidates.
	Manage and coordinate the completion of employees&rsquo; probationary forms with supervisors, and prepare confirmation letters upon approval
	Receive and welcome new staff in the Company and introduce them to all members of staff upon resumption
	Present Company&rsquo;s Employees Staff Handbook and other documents needed to be signed
	Register new staff with PFAs and ensure the recruit is duly registered for Retirement Saving Account (RSA)
	Follow up with resigned employees to receive the Company&rsquo;s asset and update same on the shared folder &ndash; Abuja branch
	Update new staff data on oracle &ndash; addition; terminate and database correction for employees if necessary
	Input employee&rsquo;s Absent hours, Overtime and others on Oracle before running the payroll
	Printing and distribution of employee&rsquo;s payslips upon request
	Prepare and update Annual leave tracker for Depots under Abuja staff and share the file verification and review.
	Prepare a weekly employee attendance repots to ensure compliance and share same with the Head of Departments
	Collate and update weekly Manpower reports and send to the HR Consultant for review
	Prepare Status change form (Transfer Form) and send to concern branches upon approval
	Receipt and maintain a register of depots keys at the year-end of depots closure.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr.abuja@jubailiagrotec.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

HR Officer Job at Jubaili Agrotec Limited




        
    

        
        Jubaili Agrotec Limited is a leading agro-allied company dedicated to providing high-quality agricultural inputs across Africa. With operations in Nigeria and several other African countries, the company specializes in crop protection products, fertilizers, seeds, and agricultural equipment. JAG is committed to supporting farmers through reliable products, strong distribution networks, and sustainable agricultural practices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Receive completed manpower requisition form and forward same to Corporate HR
	Coordinate the entire recruitment process, including shortlisting, inviting candidates for interviews, conducting written tests, and facilitating both virtual and physical interviews.
	Upon selection of successful candidates, coordinate pre-employment medical examinations and follow up with the laboratory for results.
	Collaborate with depots to monitor their recruitment activities and provide guidance on outstanding documentation and processes.
	Sought approval from Corporate HR for Salary package and relate same to suitable candidate
	Prepare and issue appointment or contract letters to qualified candidates.
	Manage and coordinate the completion of employees’ probationary forms with supervisors, and prepare confirmation letters upon approval
	Receive and welcome new staff in the Company and introduce them to all members of staff upon resumption
	Present Company’s Employees Staff Handbook and other documents needed to be signed
	Register new staff with PFAs and ensure the recruit is duly registered for Retirement Saving Account (RSA)
	Follow up with resigned employees to receive the Company’s asset and update same on the shared folder – Abuja branch
	Update new staff data on oracle – addition; terminate and database correction for employees if necessary
	Input employee’s Absent hours, Overtime and others on Oracle before running the payroll
	Printing and distribution of employee’s payslips upon request
	Prepare and update Annual leave tracker for Depots under Abuja staff and share the file verification and review.
	Prepare a weekly employee attendance repots to ensure compliance and share same with the Head of Departments
	Collate and update weekly Manpower reports and send to the HR Consultant for review
	Prepare Status change form (Transfer Form) and send to concern branches upon approval
	Receipt and maintain a register of depots keys at the year-end of depots closure.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Mobile App Developer",
  "description": "Proforce Galaxies is a forward-looking advanced space technology and defense-focused company committed to delivering advanced satellite and space-based solutions that support national security, strategic intelligence, and critical infrastructure protection. Our capabilities are centered on the design, development, and deployment of high-performance systems that enable secure communications, real-time surveillance, and data-driven decision-making. At Proforce Galaxies, we are dedicated to strengthening technological sovereignty, enhancing situational awareness, and advancing aerospace innovation to support mission-critical operations and long-term national and global security objectives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mobile App Developer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ode-Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;(On-site)
Sector: Space and Satellite Technology

Role Overview


	We are seeking a highly skilled and innovative Mobile App Developer to join our growing team of engineers and technology professionals.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing and building intuitive, scalable, and high-performance mobile applications that provide users with seamless access to real-time satellite data and AI-powered insights.


Key Responsibilities


	Mobile Application Development: Design, develop, and maintain cross-platform mobile applications (Android &amp; iOS) using React Native.
	API Integration: Collaborate with Software team to integrate RESTful APIs, cloud services, and real-time geospatial data.
	Data Visualization: Work closely with GIS analysts and AI engineers to develop interactive dashboards, maps, and visualization tools.
	Performance Optimization: Ensure applications are secure, responsive, and optimized for performance, scalability, and offline functionality where required.
	Cross-Platform Consistency: Deliver a consistent and high-quality user experience across multiple devices and platforms.
	Testing &amp; Debugging: Perform unit, integration, and functional testing to ensure application reliability and stability.
	Collaboration: Engage with cross-functional teams (frontend, backend, AI) to deliver integrated solutions.
	Continuous Improvement: Stay current with emerging technologies, tools, and best practices in mobile development.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree (B.Sc.) in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in mobile application development or a similar role.
	Strong proficiency in React Native (mandatory requirement).
	Solid understanding of REST APIs, JSON, and third-party integrations.
	Experience deploying applications to the Google Play Store and Apple App Store.
	Familiarity with geospatial tools and mapping technologies (e.g., Mapbox, Leaflet, Google Maps API) is an added advantage.
	Knowledge of mobile security best practices and offline-first architecture.
	Experience with Git, CI/CD pipelines, and containerization tools (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) is desirable.


What We Offer


	&nbsp;Salary: N250,000 &ndash; N350,000 monthly.
	Opportunity to contribute to Africa&rsquo;s rapidly evolving space-tech ecosystem.
	A dynamic, collaborative, and innovation-driven work environment.
	Exposure to high-impact, globally relevant projects.
	Continuous professional development and growth opportunities.
	Competitive compensation and benefits package.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated Curriculum Vitae (CV), portfolio or links to completed projects and&nbsp;evidence of hands-on experience to:&nbsp;hrgalaxies@proforcedefence.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Mobile App Developer Job at Proforce Galaxies Limited




        
    

        
        Proforce Galaxies is a forward-looking advanced space technology and defense-focused company committed to delivering advanced satellite and space-based solutions that support national security, strategic intelligence, and critical infrastructure protection. Our capabilities are centered on the design, development, and deployment of high-performance systems that enable secure communications, real-time surveillance, and data-driven decision-making. At Proforce Galaxies, we are dedicated to strengthening technological sovereignty, enhancing situational awareness, and advancing aerospace innovation to support mission-critical operations and long-term national and global security objectives.
